subject: UX & Research
title: The Mega Menu Pattern
description: A quick win: understand The Mega Menu Pattern.

hook.text: Hover over 'Products' on a large e-commerce site. A huge panel drops down showing 50 links organized in columns.

buildup.text: Mega menus show entire category structures at once. They work for sites with hundreds of pages and deep hierarchies.

discovery.text: Nielsen Norman Group found mega menus reduce navigation time by 37% compared to cascading dropdown submenus.

twist.text: On mobile, mega menus collapse into accordions. The desktop 'see everything at once' advantage disappears entirely.

climax.text: Mega menus work when categories are stable and familiar. For novel products, users don't know which category to explore.

punchline.text: Show the full structure at once. Mega menus beat nested dropdowns.

quiz.question: Why do mega menus outperform cascading dropdown submenus?
options: Users see the entire category structure at once, reducing navigation time by 37%
They load faster because they use less JavaScript
Cascading menus are no longer supported by browsers
